_source: AI impact assessment tool v1.0 — question inventory. Question prompts for §3.1–3.8 (risk) and §5–12 (full assessment) are transcribed verbatim from the bold prompts in instrument/guidance/AI_impact_assessment_tool.md. Sections 1, 2 and 4 are the generalist-owned threshold context sections; they are listed at subsection granularity (id + title) so the coverage checklist (TECH_SPEC §11.1) is complete, while their full prose is supplied to generalists from the guidance markdown directly (TECH_SPEC §9.3) rather than duplicated here.
_usage: TECH_SPEC §11.1: the reviewer's coverage checklist is generated mechanically from this inventory — every question_id must be substantively addressed in the assembled draft OR present in gaps.json with a reason. Ownership is carried per subsection and must agree with instrument/sections.json (asserted by pipeline/rating/tests).

response_types.risk: §3 impact areas: select one consequence tier + one likelihood tier + rationale. The RATING is computed by the engine (TECH_SPEC §10), never asserted by an agent.
response_types.yes_no_na: select one of Yes / No / Not applicable, plus an explanation.
response_types.context: threshold context field (sections 1–2), completed by the generalists; not a yes/no or risk question.
response_types.residual_table: §12.3 residual risk summary table (owned by the reviewer).
response_types.residual_rating: §12.4 overall residual rating: Low/Medium/High, computed by the engine highest-wins over §12.3 residuals.
response_types.human_action: flagged as a human action into the gap register; never drafted by an agent.

sections.title: Inherent risk assessment
sections._note: §3.1–3.8 each select a consequence + likelihood; the rating and the §3.9 overall are computed by the deterministic engine (TECH_SPEC §10). Per-section consequence descriptors live in instrument/consequence_table.json; likelihood tiers in instrument/likelihood_table.json.

subsections.title: Risk of reducing service accessibility and inclusion
subsections.prompt: What is the risk of your AI use making government services less accessible or inclusive for the public?

subsections.title: Risk of unfair discrimination
subsections.prompt: What is the risk of your AI use unfairly discriminating against individuals, communities or groups?

subsections.title: Risk of perpetuating stereotyping or demeaning representations
subsections.prompt: What is the risk of your AI use perpetuating stereotyping or demeaning representations of individuals, communities or groups?

subsections.title: Risk of harm
subsections.prompt: What is the risk of your AI use harming individuals, communities, groups, organisations or the environment?

subsections.title: Risk of AI use raising privacy concerns
subsections.prompt: What is the risk of your AI use raising privacy concerns?

subsections.title: Risk raising security concerns – data aspects
subsections.prompt: What is the risk of your AI use raising security concerns due to the sensitivity or security classification of the data or information the AI system uses?

subsections.title: Risk of raising security concerns – system aspects
subsections.prompt: What is the risk of your AI use raising security concerns due to the implementation, sourcing or characteristics of the AI system?

subsections.title: Risk to reputation or public confidence
subsections.prompt: What is the risk of your AI use posing a reputational risk or undermining public confidence in the government?

subsections.title: Overall inherent risk rating
subsections._note: Computed highest-wins over §3.1–3.8 by the engine (TECH_SPEC §10.2 overall_rating); not agent-authored.

subsections.title: Defining fairness
subsections.prompt: Do you have a clear definition of what constitutes a fair process and/or outcome in the context of your use of AI?

subsections.title: Measuring fairness
subsections.prompt: Do you have a way of measuring the fairness of system outcomes quantitatively or qualitatively?

subsections.title: Data suitability
subsections.prompt: If your AI system requires the input of data to operate, or you are training or evaluating an AI model, can you explain why the chosen data is suitable for your use case?

subsections.title: Indigenous data
subsections.prompt: If your AI system uses Indigenous data, including where any outputs relate to Indigenous people, have you ensured that your AI use case is consistent with the Framework for Governance of Indigenous Data?

subsections.title: Suitability of procured AI system
subsections.prompt: If you are procuring an AI model, can you explain its suitability for your use case?

subsections.title: Testing
subsections.prompt: Has the AI system been tested sufficiently and are you satisfied with its reliability and safety for the context of your use case?

subsections.title: Pilot
subsections.prompt: Have you conducted, or will you conduct, a pilot of your use case before deploying?

subsections.title: Monitoring
subsections.prompt: Have you established a plan to monitor and evaluate the performance of your AI system?

subsections.title: Preparedness to intervene or disengage
subsections.prompt: Have you established clear processes for human intervention or safely disengaging the AI system where necessary? For example, if stakeholders raise valid concerns with insights or decisions or an unresolvable issue is identified.

subsections.title: Training of AI system operators
subsections.prompt: Is there a process in place to ensure operators of the AI system are sufficiently skilled and trained?

subsections.title: Minimise and protect personal information
subsections.prompt: Are you satisfied that any collection, use or disclosure of personal information complies with the APPs?

subsections.title: Privacy threshold and/or impact assessment
subsections.prompt: Record where you have stored your privacy threshold assessment and, if applicable, your privacy impact assessment are stored. For example, the document management system, case file, or other repository.

subsections.title: Security risks
subsections.prompt: What measures are in place to address security risks arising from the operation of the AI?

subsections.title: Consultation
subsections.prompt: Have you consulted stakeholders identified in section 2.4?

subsections.title: Public visibility
subsections.prompt: Will appropriate information be made publicly available, such as the scope and goals related to the use of AI?

subsections.title: Maintain appropriate documentation and records
subsections.prompt: Have you put in place processes to maintain appropriate documentation and records throughout the lifecycle of the AI use case?

questions.prompt: Will people be informed when they interact with the AI system or receive outputs generated by AI?
Will members of the public interacting with the system be provided with the ability to request a non-AI alternative?

subsections.title: Offer appropriate explanations
subsections._owner_note: Owner assigned by decision (instrument/sections.json _decisions.8.5); TECH_SPEC §6.2 was silent.
subsections.prompt: Will your AI system allow for appropriate explanation of the factors leading to AI‑generated decisions, recommendations or insights?

subsections.title: Notification of AI affecting rights
subsections.prompt: Will individuals, groups, organisations or communities be notified if an administrative action with a legal or significant effect on them was materially influenced by the AI system?

subsections.title: Challenging administrative actions influenced by AI
subsections.prompt: Is there a timely and accessible process to challenge the administrative actions discussed at section 9.1?

subsections.title: Incorporating diversity
subsections.prompt: Are you satisfied that you have incorporated diversity and people with appropriately diverse skills, experiences and backgrounds throughout the lifecycle of your AI use case?

subsections.title: Human rights obligations
subsections.prompt: Have you consulted an appropriate source of advice or otherwise ensured that your AI use case and the use of data align with human rights obligations?

subsections.title: Ensuring accountability during the lifecycle of the AI system
subsections.prompt: Are there mechanisms in place to ensure responsibility and accountability by the agency and relevant individuals for AI systems and their outcomes?

sections.title: Use case review and next steps

subsections.title: Alignment with relevant legal frameworks
subsections.prompt: Have you identified and documented all agency specific legislation, or regulatory instruments that are relevant to the AI use case?

questions.prompt: Have you identified the need for legal advice at any stage during the assessment process?
If yes, where is the legal advice stored? For example, note the document management system, case file or other repository:

subsections.title: Risk summary table
subsections.prompt: List any inherent risks rated as medium or high in section 3 of the threshold assessment; explain the mitigations or controls applied and how they influence the residual risk rating.

subsections.title: Overall residual risk rating
subsections.prompt: Based on the assessment of residual risk in section 12.3, choose an overall residual risk rating for the AI use case.

subsections.title: Internal governance body review
subsections.prompt: If inherent risk is rated high at section 3, submit the use case to an appropriate internal governance body for review and record the outcome.
